review_title: Best CI for mobile apps by far
description: For mobile developers bitrise is a dream come true. With their focus on mobile they take out all the hassle from the setup process so that even users new to CI should find it easy to setup up their app with bitrise. Paired with the many predefined and community-provided steps there are very few use cases which can't be covered by bitrise workflows. And if you happen to hit a roadblock their team will aid via Slack or forum. I have used bitrise on all mobile projects across multiple companies for over three years and never looked back to high maintenance self-hosted solutions like Jenkins. I'll wholeheartedly recommend it to every mobile developer I meet.
author: Sven B.
pros: - extremely customizable - supports yaml for configuration - super friendly team overall, very developer friendly specifically - support via Slack and forum - generous free plan and fair and simple plans overall - support open source and contributors - huge steps library which you can use as building blocks for your build workflow - anyone can contribute workflow steps - good documentation - SSH onto build machines for debugging - initial setup just takes a few minutes and is highly automated - lots of free open source apps for monitoring bitrise on desktop and mobile - high availability - hosted in the cloud -> no maintenance - supports iOS and Android native as well as flutter and react native
cons: - storing the configuration in your repository is a hassle and comes with several drawbacks, unfortunate as this would be my preferred way to do config - workflow management can be cumbersome at times - workflows could be a tad more flexible - webapp sometimes could load faster - API could be more extensive (e.g. they don't have any user login endpoints as of now) - iOS builds could be faster

review_title: Changed our Clients' Businesses
description: Bitrise totally revolutionised my clients' workflows. We do testing releases daily because the overhead of uploads & App Store/Google Play submission is all but eliminated. Especially for multi-flavour or multi-target projects, it is absolutely indispensable.
author: Kyle T.
pros: Reliable, mobile-focused build automation: it is easy to set up, highly reliable, provides the technical depth needed for extremely customised builds & is intuitive.
cons: In some cross-platform projects using Ionic or React Native, it's easy to get to the build time limit & the pricing tiers ratchet up quite a lot when you get to the levels necessary to support that. That being said, the system more than pays for itself every month.

review_title: It helped us streamline our DevOps
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The Build Log feature made the team really organized and in the loop about what to build next and which task to prioritize. It's always feels Meta to talk about software helps you build other softwares but I am sure that your DevOps will be streamlined with Bitrise.
cons: The lack of Deployment Management, API and Approval Workflow.

review_title: A dream for mobile CI/CD
description: Bitrise has helped us simplify our mobile development process, keeping the builds and deployments to app stores easy without relying on dedicated, on-site hardware.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Bitrise has completely solved our CI/CD needs for mobile app development, taking any complexity away and replacing it with easy to understand, repeatable pipelines that can be understood by any of the team. It was quick and easy to set up, get people on board and a tool we can now rely on.
cons: Still a young platform, so things are sometimes a little shaky or slow.

review_title: The best CI tool I have ever used
description: We are automizing all of our mobile services with Bitrise and the process could not have gone smoother. Way easier and more fun than the competitors.
author: Adam H.
pros: GUI workflows that have easy to add/setup steps, links with most major services and business chats easily, can set a custom stack for each workflow, the UI/UX is really intuitive and constantly improving.
cons: The build machines are a little weak but at the price I cannot complain

review_title: Ease of use++
description: I really enjoy working with Bitrise. It makes my day to day job more fun and a lot more streamlined.
author: Menno L.
pros: Bitrise is very easy to setup especially when projects contain a Fastlane configuration. It has a nice style to it and great support for advanced questions turned out to be available at the click of a floating action button.
cons: Pricing change for organisation's versus developers may be a bit steep for tiny startups.

review_title: Quality of Life Improvement...at least at work
description: Spent a lot if hours (days, weeks, months, ...) with CI just to get it to run. Used Bamboo, Jenkins, CircleCI and right before switch to Bitrise Gitlab CI. Thanks to Fastlane it was getting way easier but not as easy to setup as with Bitrise. Requirement for us was to move it to the cloud and well, that is just about it. Moved now to Bitrise, worked within an hour and now everyones happy. We plan to take more advantage of some of the features offered soon, looking forward to it. For now though, got different workflows running for our branches and no real problems so far. All failed builds were my fault so far :)
author: Michael E.
pros: -Tailored to the need of mobile developers. Less hacks necessary. -Fast Support, helpful with Setup as well if needed -Price sounds high but well...not really. You get a lot for that :) -Fast reaction to new beta releases from Apple
cons: -Did not find it sooner Thats about it. Sure, got some wishes but i am optimistic that those would be handled fast once requested. Not missing anything for daily work.

review_title: Amazing replacement for Buddybuild and AppCentre
description: Excellent experience! We had been looking for an alternative for building our iOS and Android app as we weren't happy with BuddyBuild and AppCentre, and with BuddyBuild dying quite soon, we found bitrise and are very happy with it so far!
author: Chris H.
pros: Loved the incredibly easy workflow builder experience, which meant I was able to just choose what things I wanted to happen, and not have to fuss around with a bunch of yaml files and commit every change to see what's happening. Incredibly easy to debug your workflow as well, as you get very verbose information about what's actually going on! Also, very easy to setup a project, as they're smart enough to know what are good sensible defaults are for different types of native projects. This meant that once we imported our project from github, we were up and running in minutes, and just had to tweak some specific functions that we'd like to do!
cons: Default experience was a bit slow, but after talking to their support, it does seem like there's ways to speed up our builds.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Worth every single penny, highly recommended
author: Thang N.
pros: Best customization and managed process. Automate project setup and great UX are big plus.
cons: Honestly there is nothing to complain about. The whole thing is open source and really easy to extend or implement your own steps.

review_title: Brilliant CI for affordable price
author: Yevhenii K.
pros: Easy to start, but still very flexible for advanced use. Stable and reliable.
cons: UI isn't always fast enough. Strange first screen when site is loaded - not logged in / then logged in after some background checks.

review_title: How a CI tool should be
author: Balázs M.
pros: Very easy to use Free trial Free for small projects Open source offer
cons: Nothing yet
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Bitrise works
description: Basically I build mobile and react-native apps. I love the "steps workflow" editor and the way we (me and my team) can edit and manage credentials. This is more than a CI.
author: Nicolas D.
pros: Builds app it's super easy, you can manage to connect app center, app store connect, google play store, slack and even more. I found Bitrise super useful in my daily basis.
cons: The only thing that bothers me about Bitrise is the free trial. Machine are super slow. A single build can take up to 20 minutes, but I use Org Standard and that works like a charm.

review_title: Best tool against iOS build nightmares
author: Adrien B.
pros: We chose Bitrise 3 yeas ago for our iOS builds and have never looked back. Maintaining the iOS build stack is a cumbersome task and Bitrise made it some much more simple! They also have a great support, really fast and always to the point. You can choose them without a second thought
cons: Nothing particular in mind, I am not the onde using it daily now

review_title: Bitrise saves time... bitrise is love
description: We are doing rapid prototyping and bitrise is building our apps daily so it helps us to have fast feedback cycles with our users.
author: Ognjen L.
pros: Bitrise is just simple to use and its all I ever imagined and wanted from a service like this. Few clicks and you have your builds working and sending slack messages with new builds.
cons: Can't remember anything important... all problems I had whare easily fixed and we were supported by bitrise in all those problems..

review_title: CI I use every day
author: Igor A.
pros: - Ease of setup and intuitive UI/UX - Free plans enough for a pet projects - Great support
cons: Sometimes web interface could be more responsive :) Especially loading long logs.

review_title: Bitrise and react native
description: Easy to use CI service for multiple projects
author: Pablo Isidoro A.
pros: - Easy to setup thanks to starter workflows - Allows to reuse workflows easily - Good tutorials
cons: - Lack of stack customisation - Slow compilation times

review_title: Great replacement for Buddybuild
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: - Powerful features - Highly configurable - Maintaining complex build setups is much easier
cons: - Steep learning curve - Occasional service issues

review_title: Great software, great team!
author: Boy L.
pros: The response time of the team is very good! They are always there to help.
cons: Not much I don't like, but I would have liked to do 2 concurrent builds

review_title: The most versatile mobile CI/CD platform
description: I am very satisfied with overall experience with Bitrise. Especially the customer support is superb. Whenever I had any problems I received support within minutes or hours. Also, Bitrise allows us to build multiple type of projects and share some common build steps in text form.
author: Dominik R.
pros: - ease of use combined with large range of capabilities - great customer support - extended trial period with continuous support - great docs and examples - open source nature
cons: - costs a bit too high for Poland based company - hard to share common steps between projects and apps
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Easy as 1 2 3
author: Christian E.
pros: - The UI is gorgeous - Easy to navigate - Build logs are easy to read - Onboardinginfo new apps is easy because of excellent step by step process - Support is responsive and very helpful - Cloud based
cons: So far, we have been enjoying Bitrise and have no complaints.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Bitrise
author: Tymur M.
pros: - support for self-hosted Git repos - wide support for mobile technologies (including Flutter) - large number of community extensions
cons: Barrier to entry is somewhat higher compared to some alternatives
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A tool that you have to have!
author: Daniel N.
pros: Easy setup, easy implementation, nice UI/UX
cons: Sometimes a setup on React Native project is tricky but not so hard to do.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Awesome product
author: Rok G.
pros: Easy to setup, very configurable, great support.
cons: There are no cons for me so far. I really like it.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: One of the best CI tool
author: Alessio A.
pros: The ease of use and the fact that automatically recognize project confoguration
cons: I’ve found integration to be a little hard to use and configure, but maybe it’s just my lack of experience
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Bitrise: Scalable and simple CI/CD tool. Saves time and cost.
description: Overall, experience is great considering this tool has been a game-changer for our pipeline automation.
author: Kennedy O.
pros: Bitrise has greatly saved time and cost for my organization because automate the build and deploy pipeline. It's also a very easy to use and user-friendly that requires not much time learning how to use it. I also like that they eliminate user from setup and manage dedicated build machines to run the build pipelines. The user interface is very user-friendly, clean and intuitive making it simple to customize workflows design by just drag-and-drop functionality. Seamless integrations with third-party test services.
cons: Nothing really the learning curve was smooth and I had to learn with the shortest time possible.
